scenario.context: Station 2 of the day: check compatibility and ports.
scenario.mission: You verify that the selected hardware and peripherals work together technically.

customerRequest.subject: Compatibility check for station NS-ST-02
customerRequest.body:
Please check the planned components for station NS-ST-02 for compatibility. Focus: monitor ports, docking, headset and network.

We need a short, traceable approval decision with justification.

questions.title: Suitable port for two monitors
questions.prompt: Which statement is most sensible for a standard office workstation with 2 monitors?

options.text: A single USB 2.0 port is always enough for two monitors
options.rationale: USB 2.0 is not suitable without an appropriate graphics solution.

options.text: Dock or device needs two suitable video outputs (e.g., HDMI/DisplayPort)
options.rationale: This directly meets the professional requirement.

options.text: If LAN works, video ports do not matter
options.rationale: Network does not replace monitor ports.

feedback.correct: Correct: the number and type of video outputs are decisive.
feedback.wrong: Not fitting: please verify display-output requirements directly.

questions.title: Compatibility check before approval
questions.prompt: Which checkpoints make sense before approval? (Multiple selection)

options.text: Compare port types between dock, monitor and device
options.rationale: Direct core of the compatibility check.

options.text: Verify driver and operating system support
options.rationale: Components must be usable on the software side as well.

options.text: Only compare the case color of the devices
options.rationale: Appearance is not a technical compatibility proof.

options.text: Match performance data to the use case
options.rationale: Compatibility only makes sense if the need is met.

options.text: Preinstall private preferred software without coordination

feedback.correct: Correct: you check both technical and use-case fit.
feedback.wrong: Partially incorrect: please weigh professional checkpoints more strongly.

questions.title: Order of the compatibility check
questions.prompt: Put the steps in a sensible order.
items: Document approval with a brief justification
Run a short functional test with a reference device
correctOrder: Run a short functional test with a reference device
Document approval with a brief justification

feedback.correct: Correct: this creates a traceable verification chain.
feedback.wrong: Not optimal: the check should run from need to documented approval.

questions.title: Small material calculation
questions.prompt: 2 docks at 89 EUR each, 2 adapters at 24 EUR each, 3 cables at 12 EUR each. What is the total (excluding tax)?

feedback.wrong: Please check multiplication per item and the total sum.

questions.title: Approval note
questions.prompt: Write a short approval statement (4-5 sentences) with the check result and justification.
questions.expectedOutline: Clear finding: compatible/not compatible, technical justification, short test reference.

feedback.correct: Correct: the statement is clear and technically traceable.
feedback.wrong: Please state result, justification and test reference more clearly.
